description: A Middle Eastern fritter made primarily from ground chickpeas or fava beans with herbs and spices, then formed into balls or patties and fried. It is often served in pita, wraps, or platters.
core_ingredients: Herbs such as parsley or cilantro
preparation_steps: Soak the dried legumes and grind them with herbs, aromatics, and spices.
Shape the mixture into balls or patties.
Fry until crisp outside and cooked through.
Serve in pita, wraps, or on a plate with sauces and vegetables.

health_advice.benefits: Provides plant-based protein, fiber, and herbs, especially when served with vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Deep frying and salty sauces can raise fat and sodium.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Pair with salad and use sauce moderately for a more balanced meal.

dietary_notes.note: Falafel itself is usually vegan and gluten-free, but pita and some sauces may not be.

allergens: Possible gluten if served in pita or with flour additives
